reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing & before y; should be &y
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing & before y; should be &y
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing & before y: scanf expects a pointer, should be &y.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing '&' before y; should be &y to pass address of y
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Missing '&' before 'y' in scanf; the %c format specifier expects a pointer to char (char*), but a char value was passed, causing a type mismatch compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing '&' before 'y' in scanf. The function expects a pointer to the variable to store the input, but 'y' is passed by value, causing a type mismatch and compilation error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Passing 'y' (char) instead of '&y' (address of char) to scanf leads to undefined behavior; should be &y]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The variable 'y' is passed to scanf without an address-of operator (&). It should be '&y' because scanf expects a pointer to store the input.]
